meta_title: Impact Doors in Pompano Beach, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs HVHZ-rated impact doors in Pompano Beach, FL. ESW & CWI brands, 170mph protection, $0 down financing. Call the Kings of Service today!
h1: Impact Doors Built for Pompano Beach's Toughest Hurricane Seasons
intro:
Pompano Beach is one of South Florida's most beloved coastal communities — and one of its most storm-exposed. With homes in neighborhoods like Palm Aire, Cypress Bend, and Pompano Beach Highlands sitting just a mile from the Atlantic shoreline, and the iconic Hillsboro Inlet channeling powerful coastal winds directly inland, the doors protecting your home aren't just an aesthetic choice — they're a life-safety decision. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've helped hundreds of Pompano Beach homeowners make that decision with confidence, installing industry-leading impact doors that stand up to 170mph winds and the rigorous demands of Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ).

With an average home value of $380,000 across Pompano Beach's diverse residential landscape — from the waterfront estates near the Pompano Beach Pier to the well-kept single-family neighborhoods of Cresthaven and Leisureville — homeowners here have real equity to protect. That's precisely why upgrading to certified impact doors isn't an expense to delay; it's an investment that pays dividends in home value, energy efficiency, and monthly insurance savings. More than half of Pompano Beach's housing stock was built before 1994, meaning tens of thousands of homes still have original doors that were never designed to meet today's stricter HVHZ building codes. If your home falls into that category, every hurricane season carries unnecessary risk.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing — proudly known as 'The Kings of Service' — brings over 60 years of combined installation experience and 500+ completed homes throughout Broward County. We handle everything in-house, from your free in-home assessment and permit submission to the City of Pompano Beach Building Division through final inspection. Our team installs ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI impact doors — two of the most trusted names in the industry — so Pompano Beach families can sleep soundly even when a storm is churning off the coast.

section_why.h2: Why Pompano Beach Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Door Installations
section_why.body:
When you invite a company into your home to replace something as critical as an entry door, sliding glass door, or French door system, trust isn't built on advertising — it's built on results. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has earned an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau and the loyalty of more than 500 South Florida homeowners through one simple philosophy: we do the work ourselves. Every installation is performed by our own in-house crew, not subcontractors. That means consistent quality, clear accountability, and a team that genuinely knows Pompano Beach's specific construction environments, from CBS block homes in Palm Aire to the wood-frame structures throughout Pompano Beach Highlands.

Our 60+ years of combined experience in Broward County means we've seen how impact door installation challenges vary neighborhood to neighborhood. Homes near the Hillsboro Inlet face relentless salt air corrosion that degrades inferior hardware quickly — we account for that by specifying marine-grade components and corrosion-resistant frames. Properties near Isle Casino and the eastern corridors often face strict HOA appearance requirements, which is why we work with homeowners to find impact door options that meet both code and community standards without compromise.

What truly sets Premier Impact Windows & Roofing apart in Pompano Beach is our end-to-end process. We don't just sell you a door and hand you a permit application. Our team handles all permitting through the City of Pompano Beach Building Division, coordinates the inspection schedule, and stays on the job until every multi-point locking mechanism is tested, every seal is verified, and every threshold is perfectly set. We also offer flexible financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options, making it easier for middle-income households throughout Pompano Beach to invest in genuine hurricane protection without straining their budget.

Simply put, when a tropical system threatens South Florida, you want to know the people who installed your impact doors are the same people who stand behind them — and that's exactly the promise Premier Impact Windows & Roofing keeps.

section_history.h2: Pompano Beach's Hurricane History Makes a Powerful Case for Impact Doors
section_history.body:
Pompano Beach residents don't need a meteorologist to remind them how vulnerable this coastline can be. Since 1990, the city has been impacted by 10 named storms, each one reinforcing why storm-grade protection at every entry point matters. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 was a watershed moment for South Florida — winds exceeding 165mph in Broward County exposed catastrophic weaknesses in standard residential doors, particularly sliding glass doors and French door systems that blew inward and allowed rapid interior depressurization. The destruction Andrew caused directly led to the creation of the High Velocity Hurricane Zone designation and the sweeping building code overhauls that followed.

Hurricane Frances and Hurricane Wilma in 2004 and 2005 brought additional reminders. Wilma in particular tracked directly across Broward County as a powerful Category 3 storm, and the damage patterns across Pompano Beach communities showed a troubling consistency: homes with original, pre-code doors suffered water intrusion, structural breaches, and in some cases, complete loss of entry points during peak wind gusts. Leisureville and Cypress Bend both saw significant property damage during that period. Then Hurricane Irma in 2017 — while it struck the west coast more directly — still generated sustained tropical storm and near-hurricane conditions across Pompano Beach, exposing gaps in homes that residents thought were protected.

The lesson from a decade-plus of storm data is this: Pompano Beach is not a low-risk environment, and standard doors — even newer ones — simply don't meet the performance standards required for this coastal community. Impact doors rated for the HVHZ and tested to 170mph wind loads aren't an upgrade; for a home one mile from the Atlantic, they're the baseline.

section_code.h2: HVHZ Compliance and Florida Building Code Requirements for Pompano Beach Impact Doors
section_code.body:
Pompano Beach falls entirely within Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone, the most demanding building code jurisdiction in the entire United States. HVHZ designation means that any impact door installed in a Pompano Beach home must meet the Florida Building Code's Chapter 16 structural requirements as well as Miami-Dade County Product Approval or Florida Product Approval standards — the two most rigorous testing protocols in the nation. Not every impact door on the market qualifies. Products must demonstrate the ability to withstand large missile impact testing, cyclic wind pressure loads, and water infiltration resistance before they can be legally installed in an HVHZ property.

For Pompano Beach homeowners, this means the permit process through the City of Pompano Beach Building Division is more detailed than in lower-wind-zone jurisdictions. Permit applications must include the product approval numbers for the specific door units being installed, signed and sealed engineering documents for certain configurations, and proof of installer licensure. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles this entire submission process on your behalf, drawing on our extensive experience pulling permits in Broward County to avoid delays and ensure approvals move through the system efficiently.

Beyond the permit itself, all impact door installations in Pompano Beach require a final inspection by the City's building inspectors before the permit can be closed. This inspection verifies that the installation method — including anchor spacing, flashing, and sealant application — matches the approved plans. Our in-house installation teams are trained specifically to meet HVHZ inspection standards, so we consistently pass on the first visit. With 52% of Pompano Beach's housing stock predating the 1994 code reforms, many homeowners are also upgrading from doors that were never code-compliant by today's standards — a critical consideration for insurance purposes and resale value.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: The Right Products for Pompano Beach's Coastal Demands
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs impact doors from two of the most trusted manufacturers in the industry: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both product lines carry Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) and Florida Product Approval certifications, confirming their compliance with HVHZ requirements and their readiness for Pompano Beach's 170mph wind zone. Whether you need a fortified front entry door, a multi-panel sliding glass door system for your patio or lanai, or elegant French doors for a dining room or master suite, ESW and CWI offer configurations designed to protect without compromising the aesthetic character of your home.

ESW's impact entry doors feature heavy-duty extruded aluminum or fiberglass door panels bonded with impact-resistant laminated glass lites, engineered to stop large-missile projectiles and maintain structural integrity through extended wind pressure cycling. Their multi-point locking systems engage at three to five points along the door frame simultaneously, eliminating the single-point failure that causes standard doors to blow inward during peak hurricane winds. For Palm Aire or Cypress Bend homes where curb appeal matters, ESW offers a range of decorative glass options that provide beauty alongside ballistic-level protection.

CWI's product lineup excels in sliding glass door and French door applications — two door types that are statistically the most vulnerable in a storm event. CWI sliding systems use reinforced stainless steel hardware and dual-pane laminated glass units that maintain a weathertight seal even when wind-driven rain is pressing against them at hurricane force. For Pompano Beach's coastal homes where salt air, UV exposure, and humidity accelerate material degradation, both ESW and CWI use corrosion-resistant components rated for marine environments — a specification that pays off year after year just one mile from the Atlantic.

section_cost.h2: What Impact Doors Cost in Pompano Beach — and Why the ROI Makes Sense
section_cost.body:
For a Pompano Beach home valued at approximately $380,000, the investment in impact door replacement is one of the highest-return home improvement projects available. Entry-level single impact door replacements typically range from $1,500 to $3,500 installed, depending on the door style, size, and frame configuration. Sliding glass door systems generally range from $2,500 to $6,000 per opening, while French door systems land in a similar range based on unit size and glass package selected. Full-home door packages — replacing every exterior entry point with HVHZ-rated impact units — commonly run between $8,000 and $20,000 for average Pompano Beach properties, though pricing varies with the number of openings and specific product selections.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes these investments accessible with flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month, with $0 down programs available to qualified homeowners. For middle-income households throughout Pompano Beach's residential neighborhoods, the ability to spread the cost over time while immediately capturing insurance savings and energy efficiency gains changes the financial calculus significantly. Many homeowners find their monthly insurance premium reduction partially — or even fully — offsets their financing payment, making impact door protection genuinely affordable from day one.

Beyond insurance, the return on investment for impact doors in Pompano Beach is reinforced by real estate data. Homes with documented HVHZ-compliant impact openings consistently command stronger resale premiums in Broward County's competitive market, particularly as more buyers — many of them arriving from inland states with no hurricane experience — specifically seek turnkey storm-protected properties. Combine that with reduced air conditioning costs from improved door sealing and UV-blocking laminated glass, and the long-term financial case for impact doors in Pompano Beach is exceptionally strong.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings for Pompano Beach Homeowners Who Upgrade to Impact Doors
section_insurance.body:
Florida homeowners insurance has become one of the most significant household expenses in Broward County, and Pompano Beach residents — living within a mile of the coast in an HVHZ jurisdiction — typically pay among the highest premiums in the state. The good news is that impact door installation is one of the most effective ways to generate meaningful, lasting reductions in those premiums. Under Florida law, insurance carriers are required to offer wind mitigation discounts to homeowners whose properties meet specific structural standards, and HVHZ-rated impact doors on all exterior openings qualify as a major credit category.

The wind mitigation inspection process — conducted by a licensed inspector after your impact door installation is complete — documents your home's upgraded protection level in a standardized report submitted to your insurance carrier. Depending on your insurer and the completeness of your impact protection (windows, doors, and roof covering all factor into the calculation), Pompano Beach homeowners routinely see windstorm premium reductions of 20% to 45%. For a home paying $4,000 to $6,000 annually in homeowners insurance — common in this coastal zip code range — that translates to $800 to $2,700 in annual savings, every year, for the life of the doors.

Major carriers serving Pompano Beach including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and Heritage Property & Casualty all recognize and reward HVHZ-compliant impact door installations. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides homeowners with the product approval documentation and installation records needed to support a smooth wind mitigation inspection. We also recommend scheduling your inspection promptly after permit closure so savings begin reflecting in your very next renewal cycle. When you factor in the compounding effect of annual premium savings over a 20-year door lifespan, the insurance benefit alone often represents tens of thousands of dollars returned to Pompano Beach homeowners.

faqs.q: Do I really need HVHZ-rated impact doors if my Pompano Beach home already has hurricane shutters?
faqs.a: Hurricane shutters provide some protection, but they must be deployed manually before every storm — a process that's easy to delay or skip entirely during fast-moving storms like Wilma. HVHZ-rated impact doors from Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provide permanent, always-on protection with no action required. They also qualify for superior wind mitigation insurance credits compared to accordion or panel shutters, and they eliminate the maintenance hassles that come with shutter systems in Pompano Beach's salt air environment.

faqs.q: How much does impact door installation typically cost for a Pompano Beach home, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Costs vary by door type and number of openings, but most Pompano Beach homeowners invest between $1,500 and $6,000 per door opening installed. Full-home packages can range from $8,000 to $20,000 depending on the scope of work. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options for qualified applicants, making it possible to protect your home immediately without a large upfront payment. Contact us for a free in-home estimate tailored to your specific property.

faqs.q: What permits are required for impact door installation in Pompano Beach, and who handles them?
faqs.a: All impact door replacements in Pompano Beach require a building permit through the City of Pompano Beach Building Division. Because the city falls within the High Velocity Hurricane Zone, permit applications must include product approval documentation showing HVHZ compliance, and a final inspection is required before the permit closes. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permit process on your behalf — from application submission through inspection scheduling — so you never have to navigate city hall on your own.

faqs.q: How long does impact door installation take for a typical Pompano Beach home?
faqs.a: Most single-door or two-door installations are completed in a single day by our in-house crew. Larger projects involving multiple openings — such as a full-home package replacing entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors — typically take one to three days depending on scope. Permit processing through the City of Pompano Beach Building Division generally adds two to four weeks before installation can begin. We keep you informed throughout the timeline and work efficiently to minimize any disruption to your daily routine.

faqs.q: How much can I save on homeowners insurance after installing impact doors in Pompano Beach?
faqs.a: Pompano Beach homeowners with fully protected openings — impact doors and windows — regularly see windstorm premium reductions of 20% to 45% after a licensed wind mitigation inspection. Given that coastal Broward County homeowners often pay $4,000 to $6,000 or more annually in homeowners insurance, that can represent $800 to $2,700 in yearly savings. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides all necessary product approval documentation to support your wind mitigation inspection, helping you start capturing those savings as quickly as possible.
